message:
chore(tests,benches): add invocations field to NormalizedMessage constructors

The NormalizedMessage struct gained an `invocations: Vec<...>` field
(likely from a parallel change adding tool invocation tracking to the
data model). Every test and benchmark that constructs a NormalizedMessage
literal needs the new field to compile. This commit adds
`invocations: Vec::new()` to all 50+ construction sites across:

- benches/cache_micro.rs, db_perf.rs, integration_regression.rs,
  runtime_perf.rs
- tests/cli_robot.rs (comment clarity update), indexer_tantivy.rs,
  load_archive_size.rs, load_concurrent.rs, logging.rs, memory_tests.rs,
  multi_source_integration.rs, reproduce_query_bug.rs,
  serialization_compat.rs, serialization_golden.rs, util/mod.rs

No behavioral changes -- purely mechanical field additions to satisfy
the struct's new shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -107,6 +107,7 @@ fn norm_msg(
         content: content.to_string(),
         extra: json!({}),
         snippets: vec![],
+        invocations: Vec::new(),
     }
 }

@@ -26,6 +26,7 @@ fn test_not_or_semantics_regression() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
             created_at: None,
             extra: serde_json::Value::Null,
             snippets: vec![],
+            invocations: Vec::new(),
         }],
         external_id: None,
         title: None,
@@ -49,6 +50,7 @@ fn test_not_or_semantics_regression() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
             created_at: None,
             extra: serde_json::Value::Null,
             snippets: vec![],
+            invocations: Vec::new(),
         }],
